title: sorbus torminalis
intro: Sorbus torminalis, also known as the wild service tree, is a deciduous tree native to Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its distinctive fruit and attractive white flowers. The bark and fruit of the Sorbus torminalis plant have been used in traditional medicine for centuries, possessing anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Sorbus torminalis include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and support the immune system, making it a valuable remedy for various health conditions. Sorbus torminalis can be prepared as a tincture, infusion, or decoction, allowing for a range of herbal preparations to be used in traditional medicine.
